name: ElectraOFF
description: Photostable negative-going eFRET GEVI combining Ace2 opsin with mBaoJin(3M), a StayGold-derived bright and photostable green FP.

spectrum.name: ElectraOFF
spectrum.note: The Electra paper does not report excitation/emission spectra; the fluorophore is mBaoJin(3M), a StayGold-derived green FP. This is the digitized mBaoJin spectrum (peakEx 500 nm, peakEm 508 nm) from FPbase, shared with ElectraON and Vega.

kinetics.note: Values are the fast time constant (τfast) reported by the paper (Fig. S3d is explicitly labeled 'Kinetics (τfast)'): τon = 1.75 ± 0.32 ms and τoff = 1.14 ± 0.25 ms, measured from a 100-mV step (-70→+30 mV depolarization, +30→-70 mV repolarization) in HEK293T cells at 23-25°C (Methods: Tyrode's solution maintained at 23-25°C), 2.5 kHz acquisition. The 'τfast' label implies a multi-exponential fit, but the paper does not report the slow component or its amplitude, so an amplitude-weighted mean cannot be computed; τfast is stored as the best available value and may understate the true mean response if a slow component exists.

dynamicRangeData.note: ΔF/F0 for a 100-mV step (-70 → +30 mV), read at +30 mV from the F-V curve (Fig. S3b; ≈-12% at +20 mV, ≈-15% at +40 mV) in HEK293T cells under 20 mW/mm² 1P wide-field illumination (1 kHz acquisition); main text reports a full-range dynamic range of -20.6 ± 2.4% ΔF/F0 over the -100 to +60 mV (160 mV) sweep

ΔF/F change of approximately 18% in ElectraOFF-expressing CHO cells under scanless holographic two-photon excitation at 940 nm (0.60 mW/μm²) for a -60 → +40 mV (100 mV) voltage-clamp step (Fig. 3c quantifies ΔF/F; the traces are in Fig. 3b). Value stated in the main text.

sensitivityData.note: Peak absolute ΔF/F₀ per 1-AP electrical stimulation in primary hippocampal neurons under 1P wide-field illumination (n=36 cells)

apWidthData.sample: Mouse, primary hippocampal neurons, in vitro
apWidthData.note: Optical single-AP FWHM derived by digitizing the averaged ElectraOFF spike waveform (gold trace) in the right sub-panel of Fig. 1j (|ΔF/F₀| vs time). Re-measured 2026-07-04 at 400-dpi render of p.28: the 50-ms horizontal scale bar is a solid 91-px black run (x1259→1350) → 1.82 px/ms; the gold trace (legend RGB≈240,210,101, distinguished from ElectraON orange≈255,169,66) has flat pre-spike baseline topy=3373, peak topy=3315 (amplitude 58 px = 13.5% ΔF/F via the 43-px = 10% vertical bar, consistent with the paper's 14.5% per AP), half-max crossings at x≈1719.2 and x≈1731.3 → FWHM 12.1 px = 6.6 ms. (The prior sweep stored 7.5 ms using an over-estimated 126-px/50-ms calibration; a QA re-read using 101-px/50-ms gave ~5.0 ms — the actual solid scale bar is 91 px.) 1P wide-field, 1 kHz imaging; Methods: 1-AP field stimulation in DIV12-18 primary hippocampal neurons at room temperature, 22°C. Value reflects sensor kinetics + AP shape + the 1-ms frame period. Fig. 1j also plots pAce, ElectraON and Ace-mNeon2 averaged waveforms (cross-GEVI).

brightnessData.note: Ace-L3-mBJ(3M) (ElectraOFF) green-to-red fluorescence ratio was 1.6-fold higher than Ace-mNeon2 in HeLa cells

photostabilityData.note: Normalized baseline fluorescence after 30-min continuous 1P wide-field illumination at 5 mW/mm² in cultured primary hippocampal neurons — read off the gold ElectraOFF curve in Fig. S4h, which falls from 1.0 to ~0.71 at 30 min (companion curves: ElectraON ~1.0/orange, pAce ~0.44/cyan, Ace-mNeon2 ~0.18/green). Corrected 2026-07-03: the prior stored value (95%) did not match the S4h curve endpoint (~71%). Consistent with the re-digitized photobleach[] curve (t₇₅% ≈ 22.9 min).

Retained high basal fluorescence (minimal bleaching) under continuous 10 mW/mm² 1P wide-field illumination in HeLa cells, by contrast with pAce and Ace-mNeon2 which lost 80% and 83% (dropping to ~20%) over the same ~2400 s (~40 min) window (Fig. 1h). Corrected 2026-07-03: the gold ElectraOFF (Ace-N3-mBJ(3M)) curve rises to ~104% by ~500 s then declines to ~92% at 2400 s; the prior stored 85% underread the endpoint (ElectraON/orange stays ~102%).

photobleach.note: Photobleaching of the fluorescence baseline over a 30-min continuous 1P recording at 5 mW/mm² in cultured primary hippocampal neurons (Fig. S4h, the gold ElectraOFF curve — sensor colors confirmed against the labeled bars in Fig. S4f/g: pAce cyan, ElectraON orange, Ace-mNeon2 green, ElectraOFF gold). Re-digitized 2026-07-03 (HSV gold-mask column-scan of a 400-dpi render of Fig. S4h, calibration 0/30 min → x, 1.1/0.1 → y; overlay-verified against the figure): the prior curve ran ~0.02–0.03 too high through the 5–25 min region and put the 0.75 crossing near 26 min, whereas the gold curve crosses 0.75 at ~21 min. Stretched exponential F = exp(−(t/6519)^0.800), r²=0.97; t₇₅% ≈ 1372 s (22.9 min), inside the 1800 s window (digitized points cross 0.75 at ~1420 s). Companion curves (same figure): ElectraON, pAce, Ace-mNeon2.

Photobleaching of basal fluorescence over ~2400 s (~40 min) continuous 1P wide-field illumination at 10 mW/mm² in HeLa cells (Fig. 1h, the gold 'Ace-N3-mBJ(3M) (ElectraOFF)' curve; companions: ElectraON dark-orange ~1.02 stable, pAce cyan and Ace-mNeon2 green both bleaching to ~0.18–0.20). Digitized 2026-07-04 (HSV gold-mask column-scan of a 400-dpi render of p.28, axis calibration y 0/100 → px2728/2434, x 0/2500 s → px2409/2881; legend-verified gold RGB≈240,210,101 vs ElectraON orange≈255,169,66). The gold curve rises to ~1.024 by ~360 s (an early trafficking/maturation-related rise seen for StayGold-derived FPs) then declines to ~0.95 at 2400 s — it never reaches 75% of initial within the window, so no model-free t₇₅% or bleach fit is reported (analogous to the near-flat ElectraON curve). Added to enable cross-overlay with the Fig. 1h family per the ElectraON page. The endpoint (~92–95%) is also captured in photostabilityData (Fig. 1h). Cross-measured in the same ElectraON/ElectraOFF paper.
